title: Ethereum and cryptocurrency private keys should not be disclosed
description: Cryptocurrency private keys are the sole proof of ownership for blockchain wallets and assets. A private key is a 256-bit number that cannot be revoked or rotated — whoever holds the private key owns the funds permanently. Hardcoding private keys in source code is catastrophic and irreversible.
recommendation: Remove all cryptocurrency private keys from source code immediately. Use hardware wallets (Ledger, Trezor) or secure key management solutions (AWS KMS, HashiCorp Vault) for production keys. For development, use dedicated test wallets with no real funds. Never commit private keys to version control under any circumstances.
impact: Complete and irreversible loss of all cryptocurrency funds and NFTs held in the associated wallet. Unlike traditional credentials, cryptocurrency private keys cannot be revoked or rotated — theft is permanent.

checks.message: Ethereum private key detected (0x-prefixed 64-character hex). This is a complete Ethereum/EVM-compatible blockchain private key. Anyone with this key has permanent, irrevocable ownership of the associated wallet and all its assets including ETH, ERC-20 tokens, NFTs, and any DeFi positions. Transfer all assets to a new secure wallet immediately. This exposure cannot be remediated by revocation — the key must never be used again and all funds must be moved.

Ethereum/EVM private key detected in variable assignment (hex format without 0x prefix). A 64-character hex string assigned to a private key variable represents a complete blockchain private key. This key controls the associated wallet's funds permanently. Transfer all assets to a new wallet immediately and treat this key as permanently compromised.

Bitcoin WIF (Wallet Import Format) private key detected. Bitcoin WIF private keys start with '5' (uncompressed) and provide complete control over the associated Bitcoin address and all its funds. Exposing this key means permanent loss of all Bitcoin in the wallet. Transfer funds to a new wallet immediately and never use this key again.

Bitcoin compressed WIF private key detected (K or L prefix). This is a compressed Bitcoin private key in Wallet Import Format. It provides complete irrevocable control over the associated Bitcoin address. Transfer all funds to a new wallet immediately and treat this key as permanently compromised.
